page: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for GPU-intensive workloads. It features a single NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 80 GB of VRAM, paired with 12 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. The CPU configuration consists of 6 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Memory capacity is 170 GB, providing 14.17 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 375 GB NVMe SSD.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated operations. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the lower tier, as do single-threaded compression and text processing tasks. Multi-core CPU metrics, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average range. However, the server excels in LLM inference, achieving top-tier performance in prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile is highly specialized. The inclusion of a high-capacity 80 GB VRAM GPU and a substantial 170 GB memory footprint makes this server a resource-dense option for workloads that can offload computation to the GPU, offsetting the modest CPU performance. This server is best suited for deep learning, large language model inference, data science pipelines, and GPU-accelerated database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring a single NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 80 GB of VRAM. It is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 12 vCPUs operating at 2.2 GHz, supported by 170 GB of system memory and a local 375 GB NVMe SSD. While single-core CPU benchmarks and text processing tasks rank in the lower tier, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. The server delivers top-tier performance in large language model inference, processing prompts and generating text efficiently across 135M, 7B, and 70B models. This hardware profile represents a highly specialized, resource-dense configuration where the high-capacity GPU offsets the modest CPU capabilities. It is optimally suited for deep learning, LLM inference, and GPU-accelerated data science workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring one NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 80GB VRAM, 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 170GB RAM, and a 375GB local NVMe SSD.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g features an NVIDIA A100 GPU, 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 170GB RAM, and a 375GB NVMe SSD for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized server combining an NVIDIA A100 GPU with high-capacity memory for top-tier large language model inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU featuring 80 GB of VRAM
Features 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 170 GB of RAM, and a 375 GB local NVMe SSD
Delivers top-tier performance for LLM inference across various model sizes
Offers average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for deep learning, AI inference, and GPU-accelerated data science
